某（女子） | 子 = [[多治比池守|池守]]、[[多治比水守|水守]]、[[多治比縣守|縣守]]、[[多治比広成|広成]]、[[多治比広足|広足]]、阿伎良 | 特記事項 = }} '''多治比 嶋'''（たじひ の しま）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[公卿]]。名は志麻・志摩とも。[[宣化天皇]]の4世孫（玄孫）にあたる。[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津大夫]]・多治比古王（丹比麻呂）の子。[[カバネ|姓]]は公のち[[真人]]。[[官位]]は[[正二位]]・[[左大臣]]。 == 経歴 == [[推古天皇|推古]]朝に生まれたとされるが、歴史の表舞台への登場は遅く、[[天武天皇|天武]]朝の天武天皇11年（[[682年]]）頃であった。天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）の[[八色の姓]]制度においては、[[皇室|天皇家]]にもその血統が伝わっていた（天武天皇の高祖母の[[石姫皇女]]は宣化天皇[[皇女]]で、嶋にとっては曾祖叔母にあたる）、宣化天皇の直系子孫である嶋も最高位の真人の姓を賜与された。 天武天皇は[[大臣]]を置かない[[皇親政治]]を執っていたが、天武天皇の[[崩御]]後、持統天皇4年（[[690年]]）に[[皇后]]の鸕野讚良皇女が[[称制]]を経て[[持統天皇]]として即位すると、嶋も[[壬申の乱]]で罰せられた[[中臣金]]以来空職となっていた[[右大臣]]に昇進する。臣下では[[太政大臣]]の[[高市皇子]]に次いで高い地位につき、持統天皇11年（[[696年]]）の皇子薨去により、臣下最高位となる。文武元年（[[697年]]）左大臣に[[任官]]、大宝元年（[[701年]]）に正二位に叙されたが、間もなく薨御した。[[大宝律令]]が完成する直前の事であった。 [[竹取物語]]に登場する、かぐや姫に求婚する貴族達の一人、'''[[石作皇子]]'''はこの嶋がモデルと言われている。また[[中西進]]は、嶋は歴史に名高い宮廷歌人・[[柿本人麻呂]]のパトロンだったのではないかと主張していた。 同年初めに薨去した[[大納言]]・[[大伴御行]]の後を追う形となった嶋の後は、[[阿倍御主人]]がしばらく臣下最高位（[[右大臣]]）となったが、彼も間もなく薨御し、嶋の次に左大臣に出世した[[石上麻呂]]も、[[平城京]]遷都においては[[藤原京]]の留守役を押し付けられるなど天武朝から活躍していた老臣達は次々と姿を消し、[[藤原不比等]]が[[藤原氏]]最初の黄金時代を築く事となる（なお、息子の池守は[[長屋王#長屋王の変|長屋王の変]]では不比等の息子達である[[藤原四兄弟]]と組んで、王を排斥している）。 == 系譜 == *父：多治比古王（丹比麻呂） *母：大伴比羅夫の娘 **同母弟：[[多治比三宅麻呂]] **同母弟：多治比夜部 *妻：[[紀大人]]の娘 **長男：[[多治比池守]]（[[661年]] - [[730年]]） *妻：[[家原音那]]『続日本紀』和銅5年9月3日条 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[多治比水守]]（[[665年]] - [[711年]]） **男子：[[多治比縣守]]（[[668年]] - [[737年]]） **男子：[[多治比広成]]（? - [[739年]]） **男子：[[多治比広足]]（[[681年]] - [[760年]]） **女子：多治比阿伎良（? - [[743年]]） - [[中臣意美麻呂]]室 [[多治比氏]]は[[奈良時代]]においては子の池守も[[大納言]]に昇進する等、中流貴族として栄え、[[平安時代]]以降は衰退したが、島の玄孫の真宗は[[桓武天皇]]との間に[[葛原親王]]を儲け、[[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]]にもその血統を伝えた。また、[[鎌倉時代]]に活躍した[[武蔵七党]]の内、[[丹党]]は多治比氏の後裔を称した『武蔵七党系図』、『[[大日本史]]』（氏族志）、『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』など。。 === 系図 === : [[継体天皇]] - [[宣化天皇]] - [[上殖葉皇子]] - [[十市王]] - [[多治比古王]] - '''多治比嶋''' == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たしひ の しま}} [[Category:多治比氏|しま]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:624年生]] [[Category:701年没]]

'''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of {{ill|Mahito|ja|真人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, becoming {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (690). He succeeded {{ill|Prince Takechi|ja|高市皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (697), he was promoted to {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
{{draft categories|
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in *{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|ja|竹取物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Asuka period nobles|ja|飛鳥時代の人物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, becoming {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (690). He succeeded {{ill|Prince Takechi|ja|高市皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (697), he was promoted to {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
{{draft categories|
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in *{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|ja|竹取物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

'''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of {{ill|Mahito|ja|真人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, becoming {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (690). He succeeded {{ill|Prince Takechi|ja|高市皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (697), he was promoted to {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in *{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|ja|竹取物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.’</ref>

=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi clan|qq=}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainihonshi|qq=}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|左大臣|" to "|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|"
revision.text:
'''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=}} during the late {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}}, he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|qq=}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|qq=}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of {{ill|Mahito|ja|真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahito|qq=}}, the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}, becoming {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udaijin|qq=}} in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|qq=}} (690). He succeeded {{ill|Prince Takechi|ja|高市皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takechi|qq=}} as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|qq=}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|qq=}} (697), he was promoted to {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}} and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|qq=}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=}} in *{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|ja|竹取物語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|qq=}}*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|qq=}}.
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi clan|qq=}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainihonshi|qq=}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

[[sn:Tajihi no Shima]] [[ja:多治比嶋]] [[en:Tajihi no Shima]]
<!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima--><!--Tajihi no Shima-->

'''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=}} during the late {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}}, he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|qq=}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|qq=}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of {{ill|Mahito|ja|真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahito|qq=}}, the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}, becoming {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udaijin|qq=}} in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|qq=}} (690). He succeeded {{ill|Prince Takechi|ja|高市皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takechi|qq=}} as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|qq=}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|qq=}} (697), he was promoted to {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}} and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|qq=}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=}} in *{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|ja|竹取物語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|qq=}}*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|qq=}}.
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi clan|qq=}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainihonshi|qq=}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|ja|多治比氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi clan|qq=}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainihonshi|qq=}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=}} during the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|qq=}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|qq=}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito]], the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], becoming [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]] in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|qq=}} (690). He succeeded [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|qq=}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|qq=}} (697), he was promoted to [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|qq=}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=}} in *[[:en:The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter]]*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]].
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] - [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|qq=}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|qq=}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito]], the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], becoming [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]] in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|qq=}} (690). He succeeded [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|qq=}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|qq=}} (697), he was promoted to [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|qq=}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=}} in *[[:en:The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter]]*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]].
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] - [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=}} during the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|qq=}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|qq=}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito]], the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], becoming [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]] in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|qq=}} (690). He succeeded [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|qq=}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|qq=}} (697), he was promoted to [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|qq=}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=}} in *[[:en:The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter]]*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]].
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] - [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Asuka period nobles|ja|飛鳥時代の人物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period nobles|qq=}}
}}

, marking the end of his influential career.

The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] - [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:多治比氏]] into [[Category:Tajihi Clan]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito]], the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], becoming [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]] in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (690). He succeeded [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (697), he was promoted to [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=}} in *[[:en:The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter]]*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]].
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] - [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''
== References ==

}}

After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], becoming [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]] in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (690). He succeeded [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (697), he was promoted to [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=}} in *[[:en:The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter]]*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]].
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Prince Kazurahara|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] - [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.

Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=|1=Prince Ishizukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585158}} in *[[:en:The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter]]*. === Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=|1=Tajihi no Ikemori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210701}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=|1=Iehara no Onna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177826}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=|1=Tajihi no Mizumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18233576}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=|1=Tajihi no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430837}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430838}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. * Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=|1=Emperor Zhenzong of Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206607}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620877}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=|1=Dan Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368483}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=|1=Seven Bands of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}.’</ref>

{{Infobox person|birth_date=0624|death_date={{death date|0701|8|29}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|title={{ill|Sadaijin|qid=Q949008}}; {{ill|Udaijin|qid=Q1351174}}|spouse={{ill|Q106155725|qid=Q106155725}}; {{ill|Q111305238|qid=Q111305238}}; {{ill|Q55177826|qid=Q55177826}}|children={{ill|Q106156973|qid=Q106156973}}; {{ill|Q11430837|qid=Q11430837}}; {{ill|Q11430838|qid=Q11430838}}; {{ill|Q17210701|qid=Q17210701}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|qid=Q11430840}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|qid=Q18233576}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Koō|qid=Q106272496}}|mother={{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu's daughter|qid=Q111305118}}|relatives={{ill|Tajihi no Miyakemaro|qid=Q18339496}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11430835]] Wikidata name: Tajihi no Shima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito]], the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], becoming [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]] in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (690). He succeeded [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (697), he was promoted to [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|qq=|1=Prince Ishizukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585158}} in *[[:en:The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter]]*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]].
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|qq=|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.

* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|qq=|1=Tajihi no Ikemori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210701}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|qq=|1=Iehara no Onna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177826}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|qq=|1=Tajihi no Mizumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18233576}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|qq=|1=Tajihi no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430837}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|qq=|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430838}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|qq=|1=Emperor Zhenzong of Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206607}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620877}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|qq=|1=Dan Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368483}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|qq=|1=Seven Bands of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] - [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''
