<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0624|death_date={{death date|0701|8|29}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|title={{ill|Sadaijin|WD=Q949008}}; {{ill|Udaijin|WD=Q1351174}}|spouse={{ill|Q106155725|WD=Q106155725}}; {{ill|Q111305238|WD=Q111305238}}; {{ill|Q55177826|WD=Q55177826}}|children={{ill|Q106156973|WD=Q106156973}}; {{ill|Q11430837|WD=Q11430837}}; {{ill|Q11430838|WD=Q11430838}}; {{ill|Q17210701|WD=Q17210701}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|WD=Q11430840}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|WD=Q18233576}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Koō|WD=Q106272496}}|mother={{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu's daughter|WD=Q111305118}}|relatives={{ill|Tajihi no Miyakemaro|WD=Q18339496}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430835]] Wikidata name: Tajihi no Shima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} during the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito]], the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], becoming [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]] in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (690). He succeeded [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (697), he was promoted to [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|1=Prince Ishizukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585158}} in *[[:en:The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter]]*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]].
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|1=Tajihi no Ikemori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210701}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|1=Iehara no Onna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177826}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|1=Tajihi no Mizumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18233576}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|1=Tajihi no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430837}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430838}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the [[:en:Tajihi clan|Tajihi clan]], flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|1=Emperor Zhenzong of Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206607}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620877}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|1=Dan Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368483}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|1=Seven Bands of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘[[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]],’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}.’</ref>

: [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] - [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|1=Dan Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368483}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|1=Seven Bands of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0624|death_date={{death date|0701|8|29}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|title={{ill|Sadaijin|WD=Q949008}}; {{ill|Udaijin|WD=Q1351174}}|spouse={{ill|Q106155725|WD=Q106155725}}; {{ill|Q111305238|WD=Q111305238}}; {{ill|Q55177826|WD=Q55177826}}|children={{ill|Q106156973|WD=Q106156973}}; {{ill|Q11430837|WD=Q11430837}}; {{ill|Q11430838|WD=Q11430838}}; {{ill|Q17210701|WD=Q17210701}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|WD=Q11430840}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|WD=Q18233576}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Koō|WD=Q106272496}}|mother={{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu's daughter|WD=Q111305118}}|relatives={{ill|Tajihi no Miyakemaro|WD=Q18339496}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430835]] Wikidata name: Tajihi no Shima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} during the late {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}, he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}}.
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of {{ill|Mahito (title)|en|Mahito (title)|WD=Q11582848|lt=Mahito}}, the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}, becoming {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin}} in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (690). He succeeded {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi}} as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (697), he was promoted to {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|1=Prince Ishizukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585158}} in *{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|en|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|WD=Q923593|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}}.
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|1=Tajihi no Ikemori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210701}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|1=Iehara no Onna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177826}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|1=Tajihi no Mizumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18233576}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|1=Tajihi no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430837}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430838}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|en|Tajihi clan|WD=Q11430839|lt=Tajihi clan}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|1=Emperor Zhenzong of Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206607}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620877}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|1=Dan Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368483}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|1=Seven Bands of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|1=Tajihi no Ikemori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210701}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|1=Iehara no Onna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177826}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|1=Tajihi no Mizumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18233576}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|1=Tajihi no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430837}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430838}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}

The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|1=Dan Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368483}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|1=Seven Bands of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0624|death_date={{death date|0701|8|29}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|title={{ill|Sadaijin|WD=Q949008}}; {{ill|Udaijin|WD=Q1351174}}|spouse={{ill|Q106155725|WD=Q106155725}}; {{ill|Q111305238|WD=Q111305238}}; {{ill|Q55177826|WD=Q55177826}}|children={{ill|Q106156973|WD=Q106156973}}; {{ill|Q11430837|WD=Q11430837}}; {{ill|Q11430838|WD=Q11430838}}; {{ill|Q17210701|WD=Q17210701}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|WD=Q11430840}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|WD=Q18233576}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Koō|WD=Q106272496}}|mother={{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu's daughter|WD=Q111305118}}|relatives={{ill|Tajihi no Miyakemaro|WD=Q18339496}}}} '''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} during the late {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}, he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}}.
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of {{ill|Mahito (title)|en|Mahito (title)|WD=Q11582848|lt=Mahito}}, the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}, becoming {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin}} in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (690). He succeeded {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi}} as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (697), he was promoted to {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|1=Prince Ishizukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585158}} in *{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|en|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|WD=Q923593|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}}.
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|1=Tajihi no Ikemori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210701}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|1=Iehara no Onna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177826}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|1=Tajihi no Mizumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18233576}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|1=Tajihi no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430837}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430838}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|en|Tajihi clan|WD=Q11430839|lt=Tajihi clan}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|1=Emperor Zhenzong of Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206607}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620877}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|1=Dan Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368483}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|1=Seven Bands of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:多治比嶋]][[pt:Tajihi no Shima]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0624|death_date={{death date|0701|8|29}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|title={{ill|Sadaijin|WD=Q949008|1=Minister of the Left}}; {{ill|Udaijin|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}}|spouse={{ill|Q106155725|WD=Q106155725|1=Daughter of Ki no Outo}}; {{ill|Q111305238|WD=Q111305238|1=Daughter of Nakatomi no Kunitari, wife of Tajihi no Shima}}; {{ill|Q55177826|WD=Q55177826|1=Iehara no Onna}}|children={{ill|Q106156973|WD=Q106156973|1=Tajihi no Akira}}; {{ill|Q11430837|WD=Q11430837|1=Tajihi no Hironari}}; {{ill|Q11430838|WD=Q11430838|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi}}; {{ill|Q17210701|WD=Q17210701|1=Tajihi no Ikemori}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|WD=Q11430840|1=Tajihi no Agatamori}}; {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|WD=Q18233576|1=Tajihi no Mizumori}}|father={{ill|Tajihi no Koō|WD=Q106272496|1=Tajihi no Koō}}|mother={{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu's daughter|WD=Q111305118|1=Ōtomo no Hirafu's daughter}}|relatives={{ill|Tajihi no Miyakemaro|WD=Q18339496|1=Tajihi no Mikemaro}}}} '''Tajihi no Shima''' (多治比 嶋) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} during the late {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. He also went by the names Shima, Shima (志麻), and Shima (志摩). A descendant of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}}, he held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and served as {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}.
== Biography ==
Shima was born in {{ill|Suiko 32|ja|推古天皇32年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 32|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (624). Despite his noble lineage, his political career began relatively late during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}. In {{ill|Tenmu 13|ja|Emperor Tenmu13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu 13|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684), Shima was granted the kabane of {{ill|Mahito (title)|en|Mahito (title)|WD=Q11582848|lt=Mahito|1=Mahito (title)}}, the highest Rank under the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, recognizing his direct descent from Emperor Senka.
After the death of Emperor Tenmu, Shima rose in prominence under {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}, becoming {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin|1=Minister of the Right}} in {{ill|Jitō 4|ja|持統天皇4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (690). He succeeded {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi|1=Prince Takechi}} as the highest-Ranking court official after the prince's death in {{ill|Jitō 11|ja|持統天皇11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (696). In {{ill|Monmu 1|ja|文武天皇元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monmu 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (697), he was promoted to {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} and reached the Rank of Senior Second Rank in {{ill|Daihō 1|ja|大宝元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (701). However, he passed away shortly thereafter on the 21st day of the 7th month.
Shima’s death came just before the Completion tier 1]]
, marking the end of his influential career.
=== Legacy ===
Shima is believed to have inspired the character {{ill|Prince Ishizukuri|ja|石作皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ishizukuri|1=Prince Ishizukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585158}} in *{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|en|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|WD=Q923593|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}*. He is also speculated to have been the patron of the poet {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}}.
After Shima's death, other senior courtiers like {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|阿倍御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}} briefly assumed leadership roles. However, their passing paved the way for the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}, marking the beginning of Fujiwara no Fuhito’s dominance.
== Lineage ==
* Father: {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (丹比麻呂) * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja|大伴比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Siblings ===
* Younger brother: {{ill|Tajihi no Mikemaro|ja|多治比三宅麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mikemaro|qq=}} * Younger brother: Tajihi no Yabe
=== Wives and Children ===
* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|1=Tajihi no Ikemori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210701}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|1=Iehara no Onna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177826}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|1=Tajihi no Mizumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18233576}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|1=Tajihi no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430837}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430838}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}
=== Descendants ===
Shima’s descendants, the {{ill|Tajihi clan|en|Tajihi clan|WD=Q11430839|lt=Tajihi clan|1=Tajihi clan}}, flourished during the Nara period. Although they declined in later periods, Shima’s great-grandson, {{ill|Masamune|ja|真宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamune|1=Emperor Zhenzong of Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206607}}, fathered {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620877}}, whose descendants formed the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage.
The {{ill|Dan Party|ja|丹党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Party|1=Q11368483|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368483}} of the {{ill|Seven Bands of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Bands of Musashi|1=Q11546323|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}} claimed descent from the Tajihi clan.<ref>‘Musashi Shichitō Keizu,’ ‘{{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi|1=Dai Nihonshi}},’ and ‘{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}.’</ref>
== Genealogy ==
: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''

* Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|紀大人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ** Eldest son: {{ill|Tajihi no Ikemori|ja|多治比池守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ikemori|1=Tajihi no Ikemori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210701}} (661–730) * Wife: {{ill|Ie-no-Hara no Otona|ja|家原音那|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ie-no-Hara no Otona|1=Iehara no Onna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177826}}<ref name="example">‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 5/9/3</ref> * Other children (mothers unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Mizumori|ja|多治比水守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Mizumori|1=Tajihi no Mizumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18233576}} (665–711) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} (668–737) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hiromasa|ja|多治比広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hiromasa|1=Tajihi no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430837}} (d. 739) ** Son: {{ill|Tajihi no Hirotari|ja|多治比広足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hirotari|1=Tajihi no Hiroashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430838}} (681–760) ** Daughter: Tajihi no Agira (阿伎良) – Married to {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}

: {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} - {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} - {{ill|Prince Kamitsuha|ja|上殖葉皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kamitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Prince Tōchi|ja|十市王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja|多治比古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Ko-Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Tajihi no Shima'''
